name: Wheat (food medium)
basis: per 100 g whole grain, as-is (~13% moisture); representative of common bread wheat, Triticum aestivum (USDA 'Wheat, hard red winter')

components.name: Starch
components.source: USDA FDC 169721 (carbohydrate by difference 71.1 minus fiber 12.2 minus free sugars/fructans); Shewry & Hey 2015 PMC4998136
components.evidence: DOMINANT carbon/energy source of wheat grain; endosperm amylose+amylopectin. Literature whole-grain starch 55-65 g/100g.

components.name: Fructan (graminan/inulin-type)
components.evidence: Fermentable reserve carbohydrate, enriched in bran (3-4%) and germ (~2%); fructose polymer.

components.name: Sucrose
components.evidence: Dominant free disaccharide of wheat grain.

components.name: Raffinose
components.evidence: Galactose-glucose-fructose trisaccharide; free oligosaccharide of the germ.

components.name: Maltose
components.evidence: Free disaccharide; rises on amylase activity/sprouting.

components.name: D-Glucose
components.evidence: Minor free monosaccharide in intact grain.

components.name: D-Fructose
components.evidence: Minor free monosaccharide in intact grain.

components.name: Total sugars
components.evidence: USDA total free sugars (context value; individual sugars listed separately).

components.name: Dietary fiber (arabinoxylan, beta-glucan, cellulose)
components.evidence: Non-starch polysaccharides of bran; arabinoxylan dominant, plus mixed-linkage beta-glucan.

components.name: Phytic acid (myo-inositol hexaphosphate)
components.source: Kumar et al. 2024 Vegetos doi:10.1007/s42535-024-01150-5 (genotype mean 1.24%, range 0.48-1.95%); milled-cereal phytic acid survey (CiteSeerX 9f964019)
components.evidence: Dominant organic acid of the grain and main P/inositol store (~60-80% of grain P); chelates Ca/Fe/Zn.

components.name: Malic acid
components.source: Rodriguez et al. 2012 Int J Food Sci Technol 47:627-632 doi:10.1111/j.1365-2621.2011.02886.x
components.evidence: Detected in all wheat cultivar grains by HPLC-DAD; one of the more abundant free acids (after glutamic and tartaric). Not individually quantified in accessible text; present at minor (sub-percent) level.

components.name: Tartaric acid
components.evidence: Second most abundant free organic acid after glutamic acid; higher in T. turgidum than T. aestivum. Exact amount not in accessible text.

components.name: Oxalic acid
components.evidence: Detected in wheat grains; low oxalic-acid/calcium ratio reported. Minor level, not quantified in accessible text.

components.name: Shikimic acid
components.evidence: Detected in wheat grains (shikimate-pathway intermediate); trace level, not quantified in accessible text.

components.name: Fumaric acid
components.evidence: Detected in wheat grains (TCA intermediate); trace level, not quantified in accessible text.

components.name: Total protein
components.evidence: Storage gluten proteins (gliadin/glutenin) plus albumins/globulins; primary amino-N source.

components.name: L-Glutamate (glutamic acid, protein+free)
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid (gluten-rich); free glutamic acid is also the largest HPLC organic-acid signal (Rodriguez 2012).

components.name: L-Proline
components.evidence: Abundant in gluten proteins.

components.name: L-Leucine
components.evidence: Essential branched-chain amino acid.

components.name: L-Phenylalanine
components.evidence: Essential aromatic amino acid.

components.name: L-Serine
components.evidence: L-enantiomer (replaces mislabeled D-serine in current record).

components.name: L-Valine
components.evidence: Essential branched-chain amino acid.

components.name: L-Isoleucine
components.evidence: Essential branched-chain amino acid.

components.name: L-Threonine
components.evidence: Essential amino acid.

components.name: L-Lysine
components.evidence: First-limiting essential amino acid in wheat.

components.name: L-Cystine
components.evidence: Sulfur amino acid.

components.name: L-Histidine
components.evidence: Essential amino acid.

components.name: L-Methionine
components.evidence: Essential sulfur amino acid.

components.name: L-Tryptophan
components.evidence: Essential amino acid (limiting).

components.name: 4-Hydroxyproline
components.evidence: Minor; cell-wall glycoprotein-derived. Trace, unquantified.

components.name: Total lipid (fat)
components.evidence: Concentrated in germ; polyunsaturated-dominant.

components.name: Linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Dominant fatty acid of wheat lipid.

components.name: Palmitic acid (16:0)
components.evidence: Main saturated fatty acid.

components.name: Oleic acid (18:1 n-9)
components.evidence: Main monounsaturated fatty acid.

components.name: alpha-Linolenic acid (18:3 n-3)
components.evidence: Minor n-3 PUFA.

components.name: Thiamine (B1)
components.evidence: Concentrated in aleurone/germ.

components.name: Niacin (B3)
components.evidence: Largely bound (niacytin) in bran.

components.name: Folate (B9)
components.evidence: Total folate (natural).

components.name: Biotin (B7)
components.evidence: Present in germ/bran; not in USDA panel. Trace, unquantified (literature ~5-6 ug/100g whole wheat).

components.name: alpha-Tocopherol (vitamin E)
components.evidence: Germ tocopherols; beta- and gamma-tocopherol/tocotrienols also present.

components.name: gamma-Tocopherol
components.evidence: Present in germ oil; not separately quantified here.

components.name: Choline (total)
components.evidence: Free + phosphatidylcholine; Likes et al. 2007 report whole-wheat-flour free choline higher.

components.name: Glycine betaine
components.source: Likes, Madl, Zeisel & Craig 2007 J Cereal Sci 46:93-95 (whole wheat flour ~291 mg/100g)
components.evidence: Wheat is exceptionally betaine-rich; osmoprotectant and methyl donor, concentrated in bran/aleurone.

components.name: Phosphorus
components.evidence: Majority stored as phytate-P (see phytic acid).

components.name: Iron
components.evidence: Non-heme; bioavailability limited by phytate.

components.name: Selenium
components.evidence: Highly soil-dependent; USDA hard-red-winter value (range widely 10-80+ ug).

components.name: Molybdenum
components.evidence: Trace micronutrient; not in USDA panel. Unquantified.

components.name: Nickel
components.evidence: Trace; genuine plant micro-element. Unquantified.

components.name: Chromium
components.evidence: Trace. Unquantified.

components.name: Iodine
components.evidence: Trace, soil-dependent. Unquantified.

components.name: Putrescine
components.evidence: Genuine grain polyamine; minor, unquantified.

components.name: Water
components.evidence: Grain moisture.

remove.name: Creatine cytosol
remove.reason: Animal muscle metabolite; not synthesized in plant grain. FooDB predicted/implausible.

remove.name: Taurine C2H7NO3S
remove.reason: Animal metabolite; absent from cereal grain. FooDB predicted/implausible.

remove.name: Arachidonic acid
remove.reason: 20:4 n-6 is an animal fatty acid; wheat lipid is 18:2/18:3/16:0/18:1 and does not contain arachidonic acid. Implausible.

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: Preformed vitamin A is animal-only; USDA wheat vitamin A = 0. Implausible in grain.

remove.name: Vitamin D
remove.reason: Plants/wheat grain contain no vitamin D3; USDA value = 0. Implausible.

remove.name: 24,25-Dihydroxyvitamin D3
remove.reason: Animal vitamin-D metabolite; implausible in a cereal grain. FooDB predicted.

remove.name: Erucic acid
remove.reason: 22:1 n-9 characteristic of Brassica (rapeseed), not wheat; contaminant/predicted trace.

remove.name: Gadoleic acid
remove.reason: 20:1 not a genuine wheat fatty acid at nutritional level; trace/predicted.

remove.name: Nonanoate
remove.reason: C9 fatty acid is not a genuine wheat lipid; trace/predicted artifact.

remove.name: Dopamine
remove.reason: Neurotransmitter present at trace only; not a growth nutrient of wheat grain. FooDB trace/predicted.

remove.name: L-Ascorbate
remove.reason: USDA wheat grain vitamin C = 0 mg; not present in the dry grain.

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: D-enantiomer artifact; the real grain metabolite is L-serine (added with USDA amount).

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace element; wheat contains no B12/cobalamin, so cobalt is not an established growth nutrient here.

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: Microbial (Streptomyces) fermentation polymer; not a wheat constituent. FooDB noise.

remove.name: Isoflavones
remove.reason: Soybean/legume isoflavone class; not present in wheat. FooDB predicted/implausible.

remove.name: Daidzein
remove.reason: Soy isoflavone; implausible in wheat. FooDB predicted.

remove.name: Genistein
remove.reason: Soy isoflavone; implausible in wheat. FooDB predicted.

remove.name: Histamine
remove.reason: Decarboxylation/spoilage trace amine; not a nutrient of intact grain. FooDB noise.

remove.name: Racemethionine
remove.reason: Synthetic DL-methionine racemate descriptor; duplicate of L-methionine. FooDB artifact.

remove.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: Stereochemical racemate descriptor; duplicate of L-isoleucine. FooDB artifact.

missing_added: Individual free sugars with amounts (sucrose, raffinose, maltose, glucose, fructose)
Organic acids malic, tartaric, oxalic, shikimic, fumaric (Rodriguez 2012)
Glycine betaine amount, choline, full vitamin amounts
Full mineral amounts (P, K, Mg, Ca, Fe, Zn, Mn, Cu, Se, Na)
keep_uncovered: Ferulic acid (dominant wheat phenolic, bound to arabinoxylan; genuine, no simple BiGG met)
p-coumaric / sinapic / vanillic acids (genuine wheat phenolic acids)
Cyanidin, peonidin, pelargonidin, delphinidin, petunidin, malvidin (anthocyanidins; only meaningful in purple/pigmented wheat, trace in common wheat)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169721 (Wheat, hard red winter; SR Legacy) - proximates, sugars total, amino acids, fatty acids, vitamins, minerals
proteinquality.org (USDA-derived) - confirmed 9 amino-acid values (Trp/Thr/Ile/Leu/Lys/His/Val, Met+Cys, Phe+Tyr)
Shewry & Hey 2015, 'The contribution of wheat to human diet and health', Food Energy Secur / PMC4998136 - individual free sugars & fructans
Rodriguez et al. 2012, Int J Food Sci Technol 47:627-632, doi:10.1111/j.1365-2621.2011.02886.x - organic acid profile (glutamic, tartaric, malic, oxalic, shikimic, fumaric)
Kumar et al. 2024, Vegetos, doi:10.1007/s42535-024-01150-5 - phytic acid content (mean 1.24%, range 0.48-1.95%)
Likes, Madl, Zeisel & Craig 2007, J Cereal Sci 46:93-95 - betaine & choline of whole wheat flour
FooDB FOOD00561 - compound presence for trace/unquantified items
notes: Dominant carbon/energy source is STARCH (~57 g/100g), backed by fructans (~1.3 g), sucrose (~0.8 g) and minor free sugars; wheat grain is starch-dominated, not organic-acid-dominated. The principal organic acid is phytic acid (~1 g/100g, also the main P/inositol store); free carboxylic acids (glutamic>tartaric>malic, plus oxalic/shikimic/fumaric per Rodriguez 2012) are minor and not individually quantified in accessible text (null + note). Nitrogen source is gluten protein (~12.6 g), glutamate/proline-rich. Removed 20 FooDB noise/implausible entries (animal metabolites creatine/taurine/arachidonic acid/retinol/vitamin D, soy isoflavones, microbial epsilon-polylysine, zero-content vitamin C, D-serine and racemate artifacts, non-nutritive trace Co).
